background.exact_age_and_generation: 28 years old, Zillennial (late Millennial/early Gen Z cusp)
background.occupation: Freelance social media content creator and gig delivery driver
background.social_class: Precarious Middle Class (income fluctuates between $45k-$65k annually but with no benefits or stability)
background.key_experience: In 2020, their main freelance client (a small eco-brand) abruptly replaced them with an AI content generator after three years of loyal work, making Jordan realize tech companies view human creators as disposable

dominant_traits: Very High Neuroticism (constantly anxious about algorithms and income)
Very Low Openness to Experience (resists learning new platforms after being burned)
Moderately Low Conscientiousness (works in bursts when motivated by trends rather than routine)

cognition.core_value: Human creativity should always be valued over algorithmic efficiency
cognition.bias: Negativity Bias - triggered whenever a tech company announces 'innovation' or 'AI integration', immediately assuming it will eliminate freelance jobs

current_state.recent_memory: Seeing a viral tweet yesterday from a tech CEO boasting about 'automating 40% of creative roles' that made their stomach drop
